notes: Will not launch due to EAC (Easy Anti Cheat)
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: The game fails to launch with err:seh:setup_exception stack overflow. It fails even with EAC disabled in the registry which is explained at:      https://steamcommunity.com/app/383120/discussions/0/485622866440607945/?ctp=13#c3203652426720465252     I have the suspicion that it may have something to do with missing registry keys that are either not created at prefix setup, or not created or readable by the game. The error log is littered with countless lines that mention various registry keys that are attempted to be loaded. This seems to be discussed here as well:     https://bugs.winehq.org/show_bug.cgi?id=45586
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: EasyAntiCheat is probably the culprit here. By default it attempts to load it or install it if it isn't installed already. Renaming the EAC binaries, just results in a re-installation of EAC. I attempted to change the WINE prefix's registry to disable EAC but I don't really know if I'm putting the right key in the right place because it isn't documented. I've changed different Windows settings, including which Windows base in protontricks and can't even get it to successfully load the launcher. Looks like there is a thread about this starting on Page 9 of this thread: https://steamcommunity.com/app/383120/discussions/0/485622866440607945/
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: EAC  is the problem
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: Alright this is going to be a complicated report. Firstly it seems that the issue was with the graphics drivers, as the game will now start on my gtx960 with the 418.43 proprietary drivers. My friend who has a RX580 says that it will not start even when she installed the latest AMD drivers. We have encountered the same issue in Interstellar Rift, my NVIDIA card can start the game while her AMD card can not. To get the game to start: 1) delete the prefix folder ie (~/steam/steamapps/compatdata/383120/) 2) start the game through steam and let it go through the dependency install process. 3) using winetricks (Make sure wine tricks is fully updated as well as wine) install: vcrun2017 dotnet472. 4?)Not sure if this is important, but you may have to have winbind installed on your Linux distribution(Ubuntu: sudo apt-get install winbind) as the error logs Proton was outputting included references to this, but I installed it months ago when I first encountered the error so… Okay, now start the game. You can change the settings when the game loads, I recommend minimal settings for everything if you have a graphics card similar to mine, as the games' performance is very poor. You can start the application with or without EAC(EasyAntiCheat) enabled in the options menu, but when you go to start a coop game it pops up with a message saying it can not start a coop game because EAC is enabled, even if it is disabled. Single player, however, works… albeit with poor performance. If someone with an Nvidia card and a windows' installation they could test the game on, it would be nice to know if the performance issues are the games fault or, proton/wine/dxvk fault.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: I update my graphics drivers to the latest experimental branch at my friends request, and now the game runs no issue, although we are still not able to play multiplayer because the game says EAC is running.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: Following the guide below I managed to run the game except that I installed dotNET 4.5. Eventually I found that installing dotNET 4.0 alone (through winetricks) is enough to make Empyrion run on my machine. Game is entirely playable, however performance is subpar comparing to Windows. On med-low settings it could be played comfortably. I did not tested multiplayer; still can't create Local Coop because of EAC issue.

title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes:
I had a problem following the posted steps, wine-mono causes a dotnet version reading that blocks further installs. so here's the version i had to improvise to get it setup myself. 
as before, run the game to cause steam's first run to finish... 
open winetricks and wipe the prefix.
in winetricks again goto uninstall program and remove wine-mono (the other uninstalls with it, and was also the issue i was having).
now go and install dotnet 4.5 and vc runtime 2017.
exit winetricks and start the game. 
--------
I've noticed a few odd behaviours in my own testing, first the options i set did not save for a few attempts, i'd reloaded the game around four or five times by then.
it's certainly not running as nice under majaro as it did under windows a few months back, it's far from unplayable though, just more prone to sudden frame drops and needing lower settings. 
it insists EAC is active. (no co-op).
a possible issue (but could be my own fault yet), is it seems to randomly fail to load chunk data. or even some block data (the rescue module building had around a quarter invisible).
(this particular issue is PROBABLY e-sync, i've not yet set it, and i'll be setting it and sleeping, be a few days before i can report in any changes anyway.
so i post that more as a noted issue. 
you can install the dedicated server the same way, however i tried inputting the loopback address 127... (is that even a thing that works with linux?) and i got told the app is not running... so maybe i'm just missing the linux version of that address.
all in all. my limited playthrough suggests it works.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

cpu: AMD A8-3870 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Works fine after creating a modified exe. First you crate a .bat file with a text editor. It should contain: cd Client and in a new line start /wait Empyrion.exe. Then you convert the file into an .exe file. There are online converters around for doing it. Afterwards you name the file EmpyrionLauncher.exe (replacing the old lauchner file). I just renamed the original file to EmpyrionLauncher.old. Now you can run the game through the Steam Library normally. Game works fine and even multiplayer works, as long as you stay on unprotected servers. I also recommend, making a copy of your newly created batch file and place it either in the same directory or in a different, secure place. This way, after an update of the game you can just put it back and don't have to go through the whole process again. Performance of the game is fine on low and medium settings.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes:
EasyAntiCheat does not work under wine so you are limited to servers without it.

Install .Net 4 then set the windows version back to windows 7.
Use PROTON_USE_WINED3D and you will get 35-40 fps without it you will get 5 fps.

title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes:
been playing for a while now, i've posted before, so this is more a longterm update.
so far i've encountered some issues, howver i do think the blame was on the version of the game, a hotfix solved much of the issues.
initially my map was generated a little iffy, certain terrain objects like trees and rocks had a definition of "container" and was undestroyable. further complicating matters was they were sometimes invisible until you reloaded the textures...
that would then make the game run like hell till a restart...
the hotfix seems to have solved this behavior, but it still does not seem to run quite right, the game gets slower with time, the ingame timers start to misbehave, not quite in sync, some faster, some slower. some de-sync with sleeping in bed... 
i've started a new world since i noticed this, as it could be a thing leftover from my bad generation. so far i've seen no signs of anything except the slowdown, but i've only just started. be a while before i can be certain the timers are still out.
I can confirm however the game i s no longer complaining about EAC for clicking the host a co-op game. but i've not yet actually hosted... so it may still be busted just at a later point.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes:
Nothing too special has to be done to get this game running. It doesn't work out of the box, but all you have to do is select "Add a Non-Steam game to My Library..." under "Games" and select "Empyrion.exe" found in the game's files. Then you go into the properties of the newly added shortcut, SELECT EMPYRION.EXE AGAIN, and force the use of Proton.



A few things to keep in mind: 
    1. Don't use Exclusive Fullscreen mode; use Fullscreen Window instead.
    2. 4.2-7 didn't allow alt-tabbing, but 4.2-8 does. Just make sure you press Escape before doing it, otherwise, the DPI will jump up to an insane amount (it can easily be fixed by pressing Escape).
    3. There will be tearing since Empyrion doesn't have a vsync option, but it can be easily mitigated by enabling Force Full Composition Pipeline in NVIDIA X Server Settings. 
If you avoid/apply the points above, the actual gameplay itself runs perfectly. It's a shame that EAC doesn't work, but it isn't the root cause of the problem.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 3400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Don't use Proton or Wine 4.11 - Empyrion refuses to start. While 4.2-9 works fine, I've been using 4.10-GE-3 from https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases and it has been relatively stable after performing far more tweaks than usual. The first is to use winetricks to install dotnet40 and vcrun2015 to get the launcher to work. Using DXVK with RADV crashes as soon as you try to get in-game and AMDVLK is extremely glitchy, so make sure PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 is enabled. There are minor visual glitches (especially with trees and rocks) and you will need to set Texture Resolution to Max and Deco Distance to far to mitigate the glitches, though it won't entirely remove them. Empyrion (especially with the Alpha 10.4 update which updated the Unity engine to 2019.1) also uses an absurd amount of VRAM (at least 6GB) even on the lowest settings at 720p except the above settings, and it cannot be played on an AMD APU (and most likely any older AMD dGPU) with 2GB of dedicated VRAM without setting the amdgpu.gttsize kernel parameter to at least 8192. You will need to check your distro on how to update the GRUB config to enable the kernel parameter to ensure it sticks between new kernels. You definitely need at least 16GB of RAM to play this if you do not have a dedicated GPU. Empyrion does not like tight RAM timings either, and was hard crashing the system with XMP timings using G.Skill F4-3200C14D-32GTZ RAM, resetting to JEDEC timings and bumping the frequency back up to 3200mhz had fixed it for me, but I will try to tweak the RAM timings in the future to get a good balance as it has slightly affected the FPS in other games. Framerate varies wildly between 20-65 fps depending on the complexity of the line-of-sight rendering, but I have seen spikes in low-complexity areas. Multiplayer on non-EAC servers works perfectly fine, I have not and will not attempt to connect to EAC servers, as I only play local co-op with friends.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes: Still runs perfectly after adding empyrion.exe as a non steam title (double check the path in the settings after adding it!) and adding PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 to the game launch options. I installed dotnet4 with the help of steamtricks, too. I gained 28 hours of multiplayer gameplay at this point.
title: Empyrion - Galactic Survival

notes:
Here is how I got up and running in Steam:
1.) Install game with Proton 4.2-9 enabled.  Do not use 4.11-X as it will not launch.  Even if you start with 4.11 and change, I found it will not work unless you delete the proton folder and start from new.
2.) Run the game to generate proton files
3.) Use protontricks to install dnet40 and vcrun2015
4.) I use the following Launch Options: "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%"
5.) Game should now launch!
I have about 250+ hours.  Only downside is you cannot join EAC servers yet.  One day...
